title: agrostis borealis var. californica
intro: Agrostis borealis var. californica, also known as California bentgrass, is a subspecies of grass native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of certain flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The medicinal properties of California bentgrass have been linked to various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and potentially even antimicrobial effects. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea or tincture, often used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds, or internally to aid in digestion and reduce fever.
